Name: The Great Flood
Effect: On draw: Put this into play with 3 flood tokens. At the start of each round, remove a token. When the last token is removed: banish all beings at locations without a Dam, then discard this card.
Anecdote: The waters rose until the hills were submerged. No one directed the flood — it simply came.

Name: Yu's Laborer
Effect: On enter: If you control a Dam at this location, draw a card.
Anecdote: They worked without rest, eating while standing, sleeping on the bare earth.

Name: Flood Worker
Effect: On enter: You may put a Dam from your hand into play at any location.
Anecdote: For thirteen years, ten thousand laborers dug channels, their hands worn to bone.

Name: Yu's Steward
Effect: On enter: Draw a Dam from your deck. While on top: If you control 3 Dams, draw a card at the start of your turn.
Anecdote: Yi, Yu's minister, kept records of every worker and every fathom of earth moved.

Name: Black Tortoise, Earth Guardian
Effect: On enter: If you control a Dam at this location, put a Dam from your underworld into your hand. While on top: Dams you control cannot be moved.
Anecdote: The divine tortoise emerged bearing the magical soil that could absorb any flood.

Name: First Dam (Left)
Effect: On enter: Put this at the left location. While here: Your beings here cannot be moved by opponent effects.
Anecdote: The first dam anchored the left flank of the land, turning the Yellow River's wrath aside.

Name: Second Dam (Middle)
Effect: On enter: If you control a Dam at the left location, put this at the middle location. While here: Your beings here cannot be returned to hand by opponent effects.
Anecdote: The middle channel tamed, the floodwaters at last found their proper course to the sea.

Name: Third Dam (Right)
Effect: On enter: If you control Dams at both other locations, put this at the right location. While here: Your beings here cannot be banished by opponent effects.
Anecdote: When the third dam stood, the Nine Provinces knew peace for the first time in living memory.

Name: Jade Maiden, Spirit of the River
Effect: On enter: If you control a Dam at this location, draw a card. While on top: Your beings here cannot be banished.
Anecdote: The river maidens guided Yu's path, marking where the floodwaters must be turned aside.

Name: Yu's Engineer
Effect: On enter: You may move a Dam you control to any location. While on top: If you control 3 Dams, your beings here cannot be banished.
Anecdote: He surveyed every mountain and valley in the Nine Provinces before a single stone was moved.

Name: Dragon Turtle, Floodbeast
Effect: On enter: If you control fewer than 3 Dams, each opponent discards a card. While on top: You may banish this being to draw a Dam from your deck.
Anecdote: The River Earl sent his dragon turtle to carry the survey lines across the flooded lands.

Name: River Serpent
Effect: On enter: If you control fewer than 3 Dams, each opponent returns a being to their hand. While on top: You may banish this being to play a Human from your hand for free.
Anecdote: The great river serpents rose when the floods were uncontrolled, sinking ships and consuming livestock.

Name: Gonggong, the Water Fiend
Effect: On enter: If you control fewer than 3 Dams, each opponent banishes one of their beings. While on top: You may banish this being to put a Dam from your deck into play at any location.
Anecdote: In his rage, Gonggong struck Mount Buzhou and shattered the pillar of the sky, tilting heaven and earth.

Name: Xiangliu, the Nine-Headed
Effect: On enter: If you control fewer than 3 Dams, each opponent banishes their being with the highest power. While on top: You may banish this being to put a Dam from your hand into play for free.
Anecdote: Nine-headed Xiangliu left nine poisoned marshes wherever its blood fell. Even in death it corrupted the earth.

Name: Yu the Great, Flood Tamer
Effect: On enter: Draw a Dam from your deck. If you control a Dam at every location, draw 3 cards instead.
Anecdote: For thirteen years he labored, passing his own home three times without once entering.
